story.iWant: to add cross-cutting concerns (logging, metrics, auth) to services declaratively
story.soThat: I can enhance services without modifying their core logic

tasks.task: Design core middleware interfaces and pipeline architecture
Implement middleware chaining and service wrapping

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Middleware Interface Design: Framework provides MiddlewareInterface with Before/After hooks that allow inspection and modification of request/response context
Middleware Chaining: Services can be wrapped with configurable middleware chains using WithMiddleware(auth, logging, metrics) pattern
Built-in Middleware Library: Framework provides AuthMiddleware, LoggingMiddleware, and MetricsMiddleware implementations out-of-the-box
Custom Middleware Support: Developers can implement custom middleware easily by satisfying MiddlewareInterface contract
Interface Contract Preservation: Middleware execution preserves original service interface contracts and method signatures
Short-Circuit Support: Middleware can terminate request processing early (auth failures, rate limiting) with proper error propagation
Performance Monitoring: Middleware pipeline includes execution time tracking per middleware component with < 10μs overhead target

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.section: FR19: Middleware pattern enables cross-cutting concerns
artifact.snippet: Middleware pattern enables cross-cutting concerns (logging, metrics, auth) and service composition with shared dependencies without implementation coupling

artifact.title: Refactored Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Decorator Pattern with Middleware Pipeline: EndorHybridService can wrap EndorService instances with middleware pipeline for cross-cutting concerns

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Create MiddlewareInterface with Before/After hooks, implement middleware chaining with WithMiddleware(auth, logging, metrics) pattern

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: Service Composition Framework
artifact.snippet: MiddlewareInterface with Before/After hooks, built-in AuthMiddleware, LoggingMiddleware, and MetricsMiddleware with dependency injection support

artifact.reason: Core service interfaces that middleware will wrap and enhance
Context structure that middleware will modify and propagate through pipeline
DI container for registering and resolving middleware dependencies
Core service implementation that needs WithMiddleware() decoration method
Hybrid service implementation that needs WithMiddleware() decoration method
Testing utilities that provide patterns for middleware mock implementations

external.package: Testing framework for middleware unit tests

performance.target: Middleware execution overhead must remain under 10μs per middleware component
Memory allocation must be minimal - prefer object pooling for high-frequency operations
Zero performance regression from baseline EndorService and EndorHybridService operations

architecture.rule: Middleware must preserve original service interface contracts and method signatures
Service decoration pattern must work seamlessly with dependency-injected services from Epic 2
Middleware dependencies must resolve through DI container established in previous stories
Built-in middleware must use repository and configuration interfaces from Epic 1

integration.requirement: Integration with EndorInitializer for automatic middleware configuration and registration
Compatibility with existing Gin middleware chain and HTTP context propagation
Error handling must maintain structured error types established in Epic 2

middleware.name: MiddlewareInterface

builtinMiddleware.name: AuthMiddleware, LoggingMiddleware, MetricsMiddleware
builtinMiddleware.signature: Standard middleware for authentication, logging, and metrics collection with DI support

tests.standards: The framework follows a layered testing approach with unit tests using mocks from testutils package, integration tests with real dependencies using build tags, and performance benchmarks targeting sub-10μs middleware overhead. All middleware implementations must include behavior verification tests and state-based testing patterns established in Epic 1.

ideas.test: Unit test MiddlewareInterface contract with mock Before/After implementations
Integration test WithMiddleware() chaining with auth, logging, metrics sequence
Unit tests for AuthMiddleware, LoggingMiddleware, MetricsMiddleware with mocked dependencies
Example custom middleware implementation with comprehensive test coverage
Interface contract preservation tests ensuring service method signatures remain unchanged
Short-circuit testing for early middleware termination with proper error propagation
Performance benchmarks measuring execution time per middleware component with < 10μs target validation
